hypertension.guidance: This item is a disposable cutlery set and is not consumed; it contributes zero sodium and has no dietary impact on blood pressure management in individuals with hypertension.

type2_diabetes.guidance: This item is a disposable cutlery set and is not consumed; it contains no carbohydrates and has no glycemic impact, making it irrelevant to blood glucose management in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This item is a disposable cutlery set and is not consumed; it contributes no saturated fat, trans fat, or sodium and has no dietary impact on cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: This record represents a 16 cm disposable birchwood cutlery set comprising a spoon, a fork, and a paper napkin, sold as an add-on serviceware item on field survey data by the restaurant Manam (Upper Circular Road). The cutlery is made from FSC 100% certified sustainably sourced birchwood with a food-safe coating, and is marketed as compostable and carbon neutral. It is not a food or beverage and has no nutritional composition. The item is priced at SGD 1.00 and is intended for single-use dining convenience. From a clinical and nutritional standpoint, this item is entirely irrelevant — it contributes zero calories, zero macronutrients, zero sodium, and has no glycemic impact. It is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegan, halal, kosher, and gluten-free. The item is included in this food database record for completeness of the field survey data catalogue, as it appears alongside food items in the ordering interface. No ontology codes from FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT apply, as these systems classify food and beverage substances rather than serviceware products.
common_user_queries: What is the 16cm coated wood spoon fork napkin set?
Is the field survey wooden cutlery set edible?
What are the calories in the wooden cutlery set?
Is the FSC certified cutlery halal?
What is the nutritional value of the disposable cutlery set?

confidence_reason: This item is unambiguously a non-food disposable cutlery set. Its identity, materials, and non-consumable nature are confirmed by the product description from field survey data. All nutrient values are confirmed zero by definition (non-food item). No nutritional database lookup is required or applicable.
notes: This record represents a non-food serviceware item — a 16 cm FSC-certified birchwood disposable cutlery set (spoon, fork, napkin) sold as an add-on on field survey data. It is not a consumable food product. All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are confirmed zero — this is a non-food item that contains no nutrients by definition; confirmed zero per product description and editorial determination. sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g, saturated_fat_g, and sodium_mg are also confirmed zero for the same reason. All micronutrient fields (potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g) are set to -1 as they are not applicable to a non-food item and are not reported by any source. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this non-food item contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per convention. No FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT codes apply as these ontologies classify food and beverage substances. Multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi in accordance with the Singaporean cuisine multilingual requirement, as this item is listed under an Indian cuisine restaurant in Singapore.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is a precise canonical English description of the item. The item is correctly identified as a non-food disposable cutlery set. Multilingual names are present in all five required languages for the Singaporean context (English, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are present as numeric values (0.0). Zero values are justified in notes as confirmed zero — non-food item contains no nutrients by definition. nutrient_source documents the editorial determination basis.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, and sugar_g are all present as numeric values (0.0), confirmed zero for a non-food item.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is 1 (valid positive sentinel for zero-carbohydrate non-food item), gi_category is low, glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0, gi_source is documented. Convention and basis are explained in notes and gi_source.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: All three ontology anchors (FoodOn, FoodEx2, SNOMED CT) are null with match_type none and honest gap notes explaining that no codes apply to a non-food serviceware item. This is the correct and honest treatment.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and explains what the item is, its materials, its non-consumable nature, its clinical irrelevance, its dietary compatibility, and its context in the field survey data catalogue.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is correctly set to Type 2 Diabetes.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases_by_language is populated with entries in English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. All strings in aliases_by_language are verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. Alternate English names, local-language forms, and eco-descriptor variants are captured.

Verified multilingual names present for all five required Singaporean languages. Score confirmed 8/8.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — This record correctly identifies GD004257 as a non-food disposable birchwood cutlery set (spoon, fork, napkin, 16 cm) sold as a field survey data add-on. All core macros are confirmed zero with explicit justification. All micronutrient sentinel -1 values are documented. GI is set to 1 with gi_category low per convention for zero-carbohydrate non-food items. Ontology anchors are all null with match_type none and honest gap notes.
